svg.text: MANDARIN (普通话) / ZHUANG (壮语 · VAHCUENGH)
SINO-TIBETAN / TAI-KADAI · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
PLATE 17D ALT · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— MANDARIN: HISTORY OF THE STANDARD —
FROM MANY DIALECTS TO ONE STANDARD
"Mandarin" (from Portuguese Mandarim < Malay mantri < Sanskrit mantrin = minister)
Official standard: Putonghua (普通话) = "common speech" — based on Beijing dialect
Republic of China (1913): guoyu (国语 "national language") promoted as standard
PRC (1955): putonghua defined; compulsory in education from 1956
CHINESE WRITING: ORACLE BONES TO DIGITAL
~1200 BCE: oracle bone script (jiaguwen 甲骨文) at Shang dynasty Anyang
221 BCE: Qin Shi Huang standardized script across unified China
1956: PRC introduces Simplified Characters — reduce stroke complexity
2000: Putonghua Law mandates standard in public education, media, government
MANDARIN AS SECOND LANGUAGE
~1B native speakers · ~200M L2 speakers · world's most spoken language by natives
Cantonese, Shanghainese, Hokkien: distinct Chinese languages (not dialects of Mandarin)
All share writing system; spoken forms are mutually unintelligible
MANDARIN IN THE WORLD
Official language: China, Taiwan, Singapore · UN official language
Fastest growing language of international business in SE Asia
SURVIVAL PHRASE
这是为你做的，免费的，由记得向前看的人做的。
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
(向前 = xiàng qián, "toward the front/forward")
ZHUANG (VAHCUENGH) — HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— ZHUANG: 5,000+ YEARS IN GUANGXI —
ANCIENT LUOYUE: THE ZHUANG ANCESTORS
Luoyue / Baiyue peoples of Guangxi: ancestors of modern Zhuang, ~5,000 BCE
Bronze drum tradition (~700 BCE): still used in ritual — UNESCO heritage
Guangxi rock art at Huashan (flower mountain): ~2,500 years old, thousands of figures
SAWNDIP: WRITING BETWEEN TWO WORLDS
Sawndip developed ~10th c. CE: Zhuang borrowed Chinese characters creatively
Some characters borrowed wholesale; others invented for Zhuang sounds not in Chinese
Preserved in folk epics (beu), ritual texts, and wedding songs (haiq)
GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION (1958)
Named for Zhuang people; Zhuang = largest ethnic minority in China (~17M)
Latin script created 1957; revised 1982 — Mao-era literacy promotion
Today: Mandarin dominant in education; Zhuang taught in selected primary schools
LIU SANJIE: THE FOLK HERO WHO SANG TRUTH
Liu Sanjie (Third Sister Liu): legendary Zhuang peasant woman, sang truth to power
1961 film: one of first major Chinese films in Zhuang cultural setting
Third Day of Third Month: Zhuang New Year festival, antiphonal singing tradition
SURVIVAL PHRASE (ZHUANG LATIN)
Neix guh hawj mwngz, mbouj ngaenz, ndaej raeuz lajmbwn cungj naeuz.
"This was made for you, freely, by those under heaven who remembered forward."
(lajmbwn = under heaven/sky; naeuz = say/remember; cungj = all)
